rulings.Case Title: KUNDRY RIVERO VS. POLICRACIA LO ESTEVES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 22, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT POLICRACIA ESTEVES DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states causes of action for violation of CC 1950.5; Intentional and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. On a demurrer the Court cannot consider documents outside of the complaint and not subject to judicial notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-588528, CGC-21-589085 WITH CASE CGC-20-588317
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 22, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT BRIAN GETZ MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-588528, CGC-21-589085 WITH CASE CGC-20-588317 is OFF CALENDAR. There are no operative pleadings on file in these actions. The Court notes that the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers and reply in violation of SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply with the local rule will result in denials of the motions in the future. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL V. CHOPKO ET AL VS. VASILIKI SERVETA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 22, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT VASILIKI SERVETA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly indicate the relationship between the Plaintiff and the Defendant giving rise to this statutory complaint. Allegations in the complaint currently suggest potential distinct causes of action (1) for forcible detainer under CCP 1160 (see caption), (2) for termination of license under CCP 1161 (complaint, paragraph 6), (3) for termination of tenancy (see notice referring to Plaintiff as "lessor"); and for recovery of possession from former trustor under CCP 1161a (complaint, paragraph 8). However, none of these causes of action are properly alleged as to each element of each of cause of action. Plaintiff shall have 5 days to file an amended complaint to allege in good faith facts in support of all elements of one or more causes of action entitling Plaintiff to recover possession in a summary proceeding. To the extent some of the attachments to the complaint were inadvertently included as indicated in opposition, Plaintiff may remove such attachments. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROB MILLS VS. PETER DINH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 22, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT PETER DINH DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that SFRO 37.9(n) is effective before July 1, 2021. While CCP 1179.05(b) does not limit the city's authority to require just cause for termination, it must be read in conjunction with subsection (a)(1) limiting the effect of any regulations adopted pursuant to such authority. Defendant's proposed reading of CCP 1179.05 would render subsection (a)(1) superfluous. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
